title: A Wider Sabbath
scripture_text: He makes me lie down in green pastures.
reflection: Rest is not the absence of work. It is the presence of trust. You are trusting the world to hold itself together for a day. (The fourth hearing of this day.)
body:
The week is over. The laptop is closed. The phone is on silent. You are going to do nothing, on purpose, for a couple of days.

The Lord, who rested on the seventh day, is not going to be disappointed in you. He is the one who invented the rest. He is the one who said, it is good, and stopped.

Stop. Sleep late. Read something useless. Walk somewhere green. Eat something good. Be with someone you love, and do not multitask. Be fully there.

You have earned this, in the sense that you have done the work, but you have also earned it in the deeper sense: you are a human being, and human beings were not designed to go forever.

The Lord is in the rest. Receive it.

Rest is not laziness. Rest is not the opposite of work. Rest is the foundation under the work. The Lord rested on the seventh day, and He did not rest because He was tired. He rested because the rest is part of the rhythm. The rest is what gives the work its meaning.

You are not a machine. You are a soul, housed in a body, on a long journey. The body needs to be still sometimes. The soul needs to be still more often. The journey needs a rest stop, or the road gets blurry, and you forget where you were going.

Stop. Be still. The Lord is not disappointed in your stillness. The Lord is the one who invented it.

The rest is not over. The rest is the day. Let the day have the rest.

Rest is not laziness. Rest is not the opposite of work. Rest is the foundation under the work. The Lord rested on the seventh day, and He did not rest because He was tired. He rested because the rest is part of the rhythm. The rest is what gives the work its meaning.

You are not a machine. You are a soul, housed in a body, on a long journey. The body needs to be still sometimes. The soul needs to be still more often. The journey needs a rest stop, or the road gets blurry, and you forget where you were going.

Stop. Be still. The Lord is not disappointed in your stillness. The Lord is the one who invented it.

There is a way of being in the world that does not require you to be ahead, or impressive, or on top of it. The world will not validate this way. The world will tell you to hurry. The Lord is telling you to be still. The Lord is telling you to be here. The Lord is the one who sees you, right now, in this chair, with this breath, with this much strength, with this much doubt. He sees you. He is with you. The seeing is the practice. The being-with is the gift.

The rest is not over. The rest is the day. Let the day have the rest.

This is the fourth time. You have gone deeper now than you went the first time. The well is deeper. The water is still. The Lord is at the bottom. Keep going.
prayer: Lord, thank You for the weekend. Help me to actually rest. To be here. To be with the people I love. Amen.
